zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Cheney於1370年出生在英國坎布利其爾芬 迪頓時，他的父親Henry Cheney25歲，母親Joan Muschet26歲。他與Lady Katherine Pabenham結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1399年在英國坎布利其爾享年29歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cheney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Chaney   with the same etymology as in 2 below.

French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’), or a habitational name from any of several places called (Le) Cheney, e.g. in Haute-Savoie, named with this word. Compare Chesney .

French Canadian: altered form of French Chesnay or Chenay, topographic names with the same meaning and etymology as in 2 above, or habitational names from (Le) Chesnay or (Le) Chenay, names of several places in the northwestern part of France, based on Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’. Compare Chenette and Chenier 2.

variants.name: Chenette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Armand.
Altered form of French Canadian Chenet, itself an (intermediate) altered form of Cheney 3. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.
History: The (majority of the) Chenettes are descendants of Bertrand Chesnay/Chenay dit/sieur de La Garenne from Brittany (France); see Cheney .

variants.name: Chaney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any one of several places in Normandy, Picardy, or Artois (France) named (Le) Quesnay or Quesnoy, or elsewhere in northern France named (Le or La) Chesnay or Chesnoy. The placenames all derive from Old French chesnai or its Normanno-Picard equivalent quesnai ‘oak grove’ (medieval Latin casnetum).
French: variant of Chanet, a habitational name from any of various places called Chanet or Le Chanet, from Latin canna ‘reed’ + the suffix -etum denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Cheney was born in 1370, in Fen Ditton, Cambridgeshire, England, his father, Henry Cheney, was 25 and his mother, Joan Muschet, was 26. He married Lady Katherine Pabenham. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1399, in Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Cheney est né en 1370, à Fen Ditton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Cheney, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan Muschet, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Katherine Pabenham. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1399, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 29 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Cheney(이)가 1370년 영국 캄브리지실르 펜 딧튼에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Cheney(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Muschet(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Lady Katherine Pabenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1399년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Cheney родился в 1370 г. в местности фен диттон, камбриджешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Cheney было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Muschet, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Katherine Pabenham. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1399 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 29 лет.
